$comment: D-CPP-LINE-DIRECTIVE: `#line` (C23 6.10.4) remaps the PRESUMED line + file reported by __LINE__/__FILE__ for the lines that FOLLOW. Pins three things a naive implementation gets wrong: (1) `#line N` numbers the line AFTER the directive N, not the directive's own line; (2) numbering ADVANCES from there (N+1 two lines later); (3) the file operand is OPTIONAL and when OMITTED the presumed NAME is left UNCHANGED (6.10.4p3) — a later bare `#line N` must not revert __FILE__ to the real file. exit 20 on EVERY target: the mapping is a preprocessor property, wholly target-independent. RED-ON-DISABLE: drop the lineDirective arm and the directive fails loud again (no binary); break the `N + physLine - directiveLine - 1` arithmetic and 11/12/14 fire; drop the omitted-file rule and 15 fires. The `release` arm proves the optimizer preserves preprocessor-materialized __LINE__/__FILE__ values end-to-end. This is the gate on compiling GENERATED C (lemon/bison/flex/re2c/protoc) — sqlite's lemon-produced parse.c carries 50 `#line`s, and the amalgamation only worked because mksqlite3c.tcl strips them.
